title: 悬崖上的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\241-悬崖上的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Bobby decides to honor an agreement with an elderly man named Bajaj, despite the disapproval of his father, a biased pastor. To avoid disappointing Bajaj, Bobby writes a letter to Enriquez & Dolores to decline their high-paying job offer in Buenos Aires.

While playing golf, Bobby discusses his decision with Frankie DeWinter. Although Frankie supports his choice regarding Bajaj, she warns him not to let the old man drag him down. During their conversation, Frankie notes that while she has significant family wealth, including houses and servants, she personally owns very little. She mentions she must go into town the following day to care for her father, whose gout is flaring up. When Frankie suggests they might spend time together in London, Bobby expresses insecurity regarding his social standing and his upcoming work as a mechanic. This leads to a tense exchange where Frankie mocks Bobby's inferiority complex. After Bobby fails to hit a ball out of a sand trap, Frankie ends the match and drives away in her green Bentley, leaving him frustrated.

Over the next three days, Bobby struggles with the pressure of his family life and his father's presence. On Saturday, seeking solitude, he takes sandwiches and a beer to a hillside covered in ferns. He falls asleep and wakes at 3:30 pm feeling a sense of liberation and grandiose ambition. As he falls back into a deep sleep, the narrative shifts to Frankie, who arrives at a large house marked "St. Assaf" in her Bentley, carrying lilies and being greeted by a nurse.
